description:
As a user
  I want to sign up and sign in
  So that I can access protected resources

steps.name: I sign up with name "Alice" email "alice@test.local" and password "StrongPass1!"
the response body should have property "user"
I sign up with name "Weak" email "weak@test.local" and password "short"
a verified user with email "bob@test.local" and password "StrongPass1!"
I sign in with email "bob@test.local" and password "StrongPass1!"
the response should include a session cookie
a verified user with email "carol@test.local" and password "StrongPass1!"
I sign in with email "carol@test.local" and password "WrongPass1!"

name: Authentication

description:
As an admin
  I want to view system events
  So that I can audit activity on the platform

steps.name: a public model "Audited Model" created by "admin"
the response body should have property "data" as an array

elements.name: Admin can filter events by type

steps.name: a public model "Event Filter Model" created by "admin"
the response body should have property "data" as an array

elements.name: Regular user cannot access admin events
Unauthenticated user cannot access admin events

steps.name: I send a GET request to "/api/v1/admin/events"

name: Admin Events

description:
As a client
  I want to verify the API is running
  So that I can confirm the service is available

steps.name: I send a GET request to "/api/v1/test"
the response body property "success" should equal "true"
the response body should have property "data"

name: Health Check

description:
As a model owner
  I want to manage contributors and ownership
  So that I can collaborate on models with other users

steps.name: a public model "Authored Model" created by the current user
I list authors of the model "Authored Model"
the response body should be an array

elements.name: Add a contributor to a model

steps.name: a public model "Collab Model" created by "owner"
"owner" adds "contributor" as a contributor to "Collab Model"

elements.name: Contributor appears in the author list

steps.name: a public model "Team Model" created by "owner"
"owner" has added "contributor" as a contributor to "Team Model"
I list authors of the model "Team Model"

elements.name: Remove a contributor from a model

steps.name: a public model "Shrinking Team" created by "owner"
"owner" has added "contributor" as a contributor to "Shrinking Team"
a public model "Owner Model" created by "owner"
a public model "Transfer Model" created by "owner"
"owner" has added "new-owner" as a contributor to "Transfer Model"

elements.name: Cannot transfer ownership to a non-author

steps.name: a public model "No Transfer" created by "owner"
a public model "Restricted Model" created by "owner"
"owner" has added "contributor" as a contributor to "Restricted Model"
"contributor" adds "intruder" as a contributor to "Restricted Model"
a public model "Author's Model" created by "author"
the response body should have property "data" as an array
the response body property "data" should have length 1

elements.name: Cannot add the same contributor twice

steps.name: a public model "Dup Model" created by "owner"
"owner" has added "contributor" as a contributor to "Dup Model"
"owner" adds "contributor" as a contributor to "Dup Model"

name: Model Authors

description:
As a model owner
  I want to manage access permissions
  So that I can control who can read, write, or administer my models

elements.name: Grant read permission to another user

steps.name: a private model "Shared Model" created by "owner"

elements.name: User with read permission can access a private model

steps.name: a private model "Readable Model" created by "owner"
"owner" has granted "read" permission on "Readable Model" to "reader"

elements.name: User with write permission can update a model

steps.name: a public model "Writable Model" created by "owner"
"owner" has granted "write" permission on "Writable Model" to "writer"
"writer" updates the model "Writable Model" with visibility "private"

elements.name: User with read permission cannot update a model

steps.name: a public model "Read Only Model" created by "owner"
"owner" has granted "read" permission on "Read Only Model" to "reader"
"reader" updates the model "Read Only Model" with visibility "private"
a private model "Listed Model" created by "owner"
"owner" has granted "write" permission on "Listed Model" to "collaborator"
the response body should be an array
a private model "Revoke Model" created by "owner"
"owner" has granted "read" permission on "Revoke Model" to "former"

elements.name: Revoked user can no longer access a private model

steps.name: a private model "Locked Out Model" created by "owner"
"owner" has granted "read" permission on "Locked Out Model" to "former"
"owner" has revoked permission on "Locked Out Model" from "former"
a public model "Locked Model" created by "owner"
a private model "Dup Perm Model" created by "owner"
"owner" has granted "read" permission on "Dup Perm Model" to "grantee"
"owner" grants "read" permission on "Dup Perm Model" to "grantee"

elements.name: Grant admin permission allows managing permissions

steps.name: a private model "Delegated Model" created by "owner"
"owner" has granted "admin" permission on "Delegated Model" to "delegate"

name: Model Permissions

description:
As a model author
  I want to tag model versions
  So that models are discoverable by topic

elements.name: Add a tag to a model's current version

steps.name: a public model "Tagged Model" created by the current user
a version "v1" for "Tagged Model" with title "Initial"
I add tag "ecology" to model "Tagged Model"
the response body should have property "tagId"
the response body property "tagName" should equal "ecology"

elements.name: Add multiple tags to a model

steps.name: a public model "Multi Tag Model" created by the current user
a version "v1" for "Multi Tag Model" with title "Initial"
I add tag "simulation" to model "Multi Tag Model"
I add tag "netlogo" to model "Multi Tag Model"

elements.name: Cannot add the same tag twice

steps.name: a public model "Dup Tag Model" created by the current user
a version "v1" for "Dup Tag Model" with title "Initial"
tag "duplicate" has been added to model "Dup Tag Model"
I add tag "duplicate" to model "Dup Tag Model"

elements.name: Remove a tag from a model

steps.name: a public model "Untagged Model" created by the current user
a version "v1" for "Untagged Model" with title "Initial"
tag "removable" has been added to model "Untagged Model"
I remove tag "removable" from model "Untagged Model"

elements.name: List tags for a specific version

steps.name: a public model "Version Tags" created by the current user
a version "v1" for "Version Tags" with title "First"
tag "biology" has been added to model "Version Tags"
I list tags for version 1 of model "Version Tags"
the response body should be an array
a private model "Protected Model" created by "owner"
a version "v1" for "Protected Model" with title "Secret"

elements.name: Adding a tag creates the tag if it does not exist

steps.name: a public model "New Tag Model" created by the current user
a version "v1" for "New Tag Model" with title "Initial"
I add tag "brand-new-tag" to model "New Tag Model"
I send a GET request to "/api/v1/tags?q=brand-new"

name: Model Version Tags

description:
As a model author
  I want to manage model versions
  So that I can track changes to my models over time

elements.name: Create a version for a model

steps.name: a public model "My Model" created by the current user
I create a version for "My Model" with title "Initial Version"
the response body should have property "id"

elements.name: Create a second version finalizes the first

steps.name: a public model "Evolving Model" created by the current user
a version "v1" for "Evolving Model" with title "First Draft"
I create a version for "Evolving Model" with title "Second Draft"

elements.name: List versions of a model with one version

steps.name: a public model "Versioned Model" created by the current user
a version "v1" for "Versioned Model" with title "Release 1"
I list versions of the model "Versioned Model"
the response body should have property "data" as an array
the response body property "data" should have length 1

elements.name: List versions returns multiple versions in order

steps.name: a public model "Multi Version" created by the current user
a version "v1" for "Multi Version" with title "Alpha"
a version "v2" for "Multi Version" with title "Beta"
I list versions of the model "Multi Version"
the response body property "data" should have length 2

elements.name: Get a specific version by number

steps.name: a public model "Specific Model" created by the current user
a version "v1" for "Specific Model" with title "The Version"
I get version 1 of the model "Specific Model"
the response body property "title" should equal "The Version"
a public model "Editable Model" created by the current user
a version "v1" for "Editable Model" with title "Old Title"
I update the current version of "Editable Model" with title "New Title"
a public model "Described Model" created by the current user
a version "v1" for "Described Model" with title "Some Title"
I update the current version of "Described Model" with description "A detailed description"

elements.name: Creating a version requires write permission

steps.name: a private model "Locked Model" created by "owner"
"stranger" creates a version for "Locked Model" with title "Nope"
a private model "Private Model" created by "owner"
"stranger" lists versions of the model "Private Model"

elements.name: Updating a version requires write access

steps.name: a public model "Guarded Model" created by "owner"
a version "v1" for "Guarded Model" with title "Original"
"stranger" updates the current version of "Guarded Model" with title "Hacked"

elements.name: List versions of a model with no versions returns empty

steps.name: a public model "Empty Model" created by the current user
I list versions of the model "Empty Model"
the response body should have property "data" as an array
the response body property "data" should have length 0

name: Model Versions

description:
As a user
  I want to manage models
  So that I can create, view, update, and delete models

steps.name: I create a model with title "Wolf Sheep Predation"
the response body should have property "id"
I send a POST request to "/api/v1/models" with JSON body:
a public model "Ants" created by the current user
the response body should have property "id"
a public model "Model A" created by the current user
a public model "Model B" created by the current user
I send a GET request to "/api/v1/models"
the response body should have property "data" as an array
a public model "My Model" created by the current user
I update the model "My Model" with visibility "private"
a public model "To Delete" created by the current user

elements.name: Cannot access a private model without permission

steps.name: a private model "Secret Model" created by "owner"
a public model "Climate Change Sim" created by the current user
I send a GET request to "/api/v1/models?keyword=Climate"
the response body should have property "data" as an array
a public model "Original" created by the current user
I fork the model "Original" with title "My Fork"
the response body should have property "id"

name: Model Management

description:
As a user
  I want to browse and search tags
  So that I can discover models by topic

steps.name: I send a GET request to "/api/v1/tags"
the response body should have property "data" as an array
I send a GET request to "/api/v1/tags?q=bio"
the response body should have property "data" as an array
the response body property "name" should equal "physics"
I send a GET request to "/api/v1/tags/nonexistent-tag-xyz"

name: Tag Management

description:
As a user
  I want to manage user profiles
  So that I can view and update user information

steps.name: I send a GET request to "/api/v1/users"
the response body should have property "data" as an array
the response body should have property "count"
I get the current user's profile
the response body should have property "id"
the response body should have property "email"
I update the current user with userKind "student"
I send a PATCH request to "/api/v1/users/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000" with body:
I delete the current user's account

elements.name: Private profile returns only public fields to other users

steps.name: the response body should have property "name"
the response body should have property "isProfilePublic" equal to false
the response body should not have property "email"
the response body should not have property "image"
the response body should not have property "systemRole"
the response body should not have property "userKind"

elements.name: Private profile returns full fields to the owner

steps.name: I get the current user's profile
the response body should have property "email"
the response body should have property "isProfilePublic" equal to false

elements.name: Public profile returns full fields to other users

steps.name: the response body should have property "email"
the response body should have property "isProfilePublic" equal to true

name: User Management
